titleStmt.title: SII 23.190: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 190. (A.R. No. 190 of 1907.) Tribhuvanam, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District Kampaharēśvara temple—on the same wall

summary.p: This is a Sanskrit inscription in Grantha of the reign of Kulōttuṅga-Chōḷadēva (III) who is stated to have won victories over the kings of Simhaḷa and Kēraḷa and over Vīra-Pāṇḍya after destroying his capital city Madura, and to have performed the anointment of heroes. He is also eulogised for his strength of arms and for his proficiency in arts. The record states that he built (renovated ?) the maṇḍapa for God Sabhāpati, the gōpura over the shrines of Śrīpati (Vishṇu) and of Girijā (Pārvati), the prākāra and the golden hall (at Chidambaram ?); the temple for Ēkāmrēśvara (at Kāñchīpuram); for Hālāsya (at Madurai); for Madhyārjuna (at Tiruviḍaimarudūr); for Rājarājēśvara (at Dārāśuram ?); for Valmīkēśvara (at Tiruvārūr) for whom he also constructed a golden hall and a gōpura; and finally for Tribhuvanavīrēśvara (at Tribhuvanam) for whom he built this temple of great beauty with several shrines and tall vimānas. He got the god and goddess consecrated in the temple by his guru Sōmēśvara (also called Īśvara-Śiva) son of Śrīkaṇṭha-Śambhu. This teacher Sōmēśvara was a great scholar well-versed in the eighteen vidyās (Śaiva-darśanas) and capable of expounding the glory of Śiva as taught in the Upanishads. He is said to have written a work called the Siddhāntaratnākara. He is surmised by Mr.Venkayya to be identical with Īśānaśiva the author of Siddhāntasāra (An. Rept. 1908, Part II, para 64).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: 1. svasti śrī . ācakrā mānurādhī samaraśirasi [ya]ssaṁharan siṁhaḷe[ndr]ā nyojaiṣit keraḷeśaṁ vijitasurapatiṁ yo'vadhī dvīra[pāṇḍyam] [.*] yo-vā vīrāmiṣekaṁ vyatanuta madhurāṁ pāṇḍyaguptā mmathitvā rājā trailokyavīrassa bhuvivijayate śrikulottuṁgacoḷa [.*] . . viśvaṁbharā yasya bhujārggaḷāyāṁ hatopasarggaṁ paripālayantyām [.*] prīsyā smaratyādi barāhadaṁṣṭrāvāsāsidhārāvratavedanā[nām] [.*] bhujapratāpā[nala]dhūmalekhā

2. saṁgrāmalakṣmīkabarīkalāpaḥ [*] trailokyarakṣāvidhi viśvamūrtte rjjāgartti yasyānuguṇaḥ kṛpāṇaḥ [.*] dyāvābhuvau vyāpya vijṛmbamāṇā niraṁkuśaṁ rājya••••••• [.*] rayāṁgapāṇe ranukurvatīva mūrtteraparyyāptajagatrayāyāḥ . . saundarye madanastaruviṁtaraṇe divya kṣamāyāṁ kṣiti[ḥ] kroghe daṇḍadharaḥ parākramavidhau tau jāmadagnypṛrjjunau [*] nītau [śu]krabṛhaspatī surapati strailokyarakṣākrame saṁgīte bharato muni rnnarapati[rdhī]raḥ kathaṁ varpṇyate ..

3. sabhāpatermmaṇḍapamagravarti girīndrajā veśmani gopurañca prākāraharmmyañca [vi]dhā[ya] hemantadekabhakto mumude narendraḥ . . ekāmre[śva]ra divyadhāma madhurā hālāhalāsyālayaṁ śrīmadhyārjjunadhāma dhāma ca�tathā śrīrājarājeśvaram valmīkeśvara veśmaceti ni[khi]lannirmmāya haimannṛpo valmīkādhipateścakāra ca [sa]bhāndivyām mahat gopuram . . jitvā pṛkṣ�īntribhutanavīra ścaturantāñcakre sthānantribhuvanavīreśvarametat nānāharmmyaprakaṭitaśobham bahurathyaṁ haimottuṅga pravaravimāna syagitā[rkam] [.*]

4. śrīkaṇṭhaśambhu tanayena patirnnṛpāṇām pāṇḍyārirīśvaraśivena mai••••••• smin someśvareṇa guruṇā bhuvanasya pitro[ḥ*] śreṣṭhāmakārayadasau śivayoḥ pratiṣṭhām . . vidyā yena manīṣiṇā nikaṣitā vispaṣṭamaṣṭādaśa sthāṇoryyena viviktamaupaniṣadaṁ viśvādhikatvaṁ vibhoḥ yenākāri ca śaivadarśanadṛśā siddhāntaratnākaro rājño yasya gurussa īśvaraśivaḥ kastasya mṛgyo guṇaḥ .
Digital edition of SII 23.190 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
